data_vintage.note: DA-RT P(DA>RT) Smartbidder file absent — Date column only. P estimates reconstructed from first principles.

meta.leakage_check: COMPLIANT — all sources pre 2026-05-24 10:00 CT DAM cutoff
meta.spread_sign_convention: spread = DA - RT; positive => DA expensive => Short DA / Long RT signal
meta.holiday_flag: Memorial Day (US federal holiday) — Monday load depression risk 2-5% below forecast
meta.confidence_discount_reason: Smartbidder P(DA>RT) file absent; all P estimates reconstructed without calibrated probability model

positions.rationale: P(DA<RT) ~56% at HB_NORTH; net load 23,304 MW (Yes); solar 19,891 MW; GR_WEST 3,088 MW (not near-zero but still moderately low); WEST_TO_NORTH export constraint MEDIUM probability; DA $16.87 deeply suppressed by solar bidding; if constraint binds, HB_NORTH RT clears above DA
positions.risk_note: GR_WEST wind 3,088 MW is 4.6x higher than 05-24 trough; constraint less likely to bind; max loss ~$50 if spread -$2 adverse

positions.rationale: Net load trough 20,931 MW (Yes) / 24,855 MW (Enverus) — divergence is largest uncertainty; GR_WEST 2,892 MW (trough); solar 25,916 MW; WEST_TO_NORTH MEDIUM; if Yes Energy NL model is more accurate, binding probability rises toward HIGH; HB_NORTH import-receiving side benefits from positive lambda under constraint
positions.risk_note: If Enverus NL trough (24,855 MW) is accurate, constraint may not bind; 4 GW Yes-Enverus gap is key calibration risk

positions.rationale: Solar 27,283 MW, GR_WEST 2,817 MW; net load 23,774 MW; Monday load 57,792 MW is rapidly absorbing surplus vs Sunday; constraint easing from HE10 but signal still valid; P(DA<RT) ~57%
positions.risk_note: Signal attenuating from HE10 peak; Monday load absorption reduces constraint severity faster than Sunday equivalent

positions.rationale: Solar peak 28,503 MW (highest of day); net load 26,288 MW; Monday load 61,918 MW rising fast — by HE13 load is 64,638 MW; P at bottom of MED range; includes the Yes Energy solar peak signal; constraint pressure near zero by HE13
positions.risk_note: Lowest P of the solar block; load recovery may fully absorb West surplus before constraint binds; borderline entry

positions.rationale: DA $49.52 reflects DAM scarcity insurance for duck-curve ramp onset (net load +7,886 MW this hour); HOUSTON/SOUTH IMPORT constraint HIGH probability (~30-40%); Memorial Day + wind building (GR_WEST 6,217 MW, Enverus likely higher) suppresses RT; P(DA>RT) ~62% reconstructed; +20% spread bias correction applied from 2026-05-22 learning
positions.risk_note: Ramp onset RT spike risk if Monday demand materializes at full forecast; solar still 17,783 MW providing some RT suppression; key monitoring hour

positions.rationale: Highest DA price of day ($59.67); solar collapsed to 6,906 MW; GR_WEST wind 6,793 MW rising; Enverus wind 13-14 GW at HE20 would further suppress RT; net load 48,742 MW; Memorial Day demand depression reduces likelihood of RT spike matching DA; primary P&L hour; +20% spread bias correction applied
positions.risk_note: Highest dollar-value position. If ORDC scarcity fires (resource cap out 17,294 MW + surprise outage), RT can spike above $59.67. Memorial Day load moderation is the primary hedge against tail risk.

positions.rationale: Net load peak 52,767 MW (Yes) — highest of day; DA steps down to $45.29 from HE20 $59.67; wind 11,689 MW GR_WEST strong; Monday structural edge absent (vs Sunday prior cycle); P(DA>RT) ~61% vs 67.5% on Sunday (Monday DAM efficiency -6pp); spread $5 with +20% bias correction
positions.risk_note: Monday DAM more liquid than Sunday — Sunday structural overprice edge not applicable; P conservative adjustment from prior cycle. Highest net load hour but wind building reduces scarcity risk.

positions.rationale: P(DA>RT) ~59%; below 60% HIGH threshold; wind 13,040 MW strongly rising — RT suppressed; net load 48,905 MW declining; Houston import constraint easing but basis still positive +$2-$3; sized down to 25 MW MED
positions.risk_note: Wind source uncertainty: Yes Energy 13,040 MW vs Enverus potentially 16,000 MW at HE22; Enverus scenario further suppresses RT (bullish for position), Yes Energy scenario narrows spread

skipped_hours.HE01-08: Overnight wind 6,723-10,625 MW (Yes) / Enverus STPF up to 12,197 MW; DA $17-30; spread thin and direction uncertain; 2.3 GW wind source gap creates ambiguity; no Smartbidder P confirmation; overnight positions lack settlement history for validation
skipped_hours.HE13: P ~55% at threshold; Monday load 64,638 MW absorbing West surplus rapidly; constraint easing; spread below $1.50 minimum
skipped_hours.HE14-17: P 52-55%, spread thin; transition hours; solar declining but still abundant (23-28 GW); load peak absorbing surplus
skipped_hours.HE18: Ramp onset RT spike risk preserved from 2026-05-23 reasoning; gross load 72,549 MW peak; solar 23,917 MW (still substantial); net load 36,330 MW low; RT spike asymmetric loss at ramp entry — yield to cleaner HE19 entry
skipped_hours.HE23-24: DA $28-29; wind 14,071-14,453 MW (Yes) / Enverus 16,000+ MW; spread too thin at these DA levels; direction ambiguous (strong wind suppresses both DA and RT)

risk_factors.memorial_day_load: 2-5% load depression below forecast possible; helps Short DA (RT suppressed) but could reduce Houston import constraint binding probability
risk_factors.wind_source_gap_2_3gw: Yes Energy 7,687 MW avg vs Enverus 10,016 MW / AG2 10,300 MW; Enverus/AG2 scenario amplifies Short DA spread for HE19-22; Yes Energy scenario narrows spread
risk_factors.west_to_north_attenuation: GR_WEST 2,749-3,089 MW trough is 4.6x higher than 05-24; Long DA thesis attenuated; P constraint binding MEDIUM not HIGH
risk_factors.ordc_tail_risk: Resource cap out 17,294-17,311 MW at HE19-21; unplanned outage could trigger 5-min RT spike > $100; worst case for Short DA HE19-21
risk_factors.no_smartbidder_probability: P(DA>RT) file absent; all P estimates reconstructed; 0.80 confidence discount applied to risk-adjusted EV
risk_factors.monday_dam_efficiency: Monday DAM more liquid than Sunday — no Sunday structural overprice edge; P estimates reduced -5 to -6pp vs prior Sunday cycle
risk_factors.yes_enverus_net_load_gap_he10: 20,931 MW (Yes) vs 24,855 MW (Enverus) — 4 GW divergence is primary calibration uncertainty for Long DA solar block positions
